bonuses.leadin: This man created a Portrait of Painter Bazille and in his old age, he hired Richard Guino to follow his sculpting instructions. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this French painter of Luncheon of the Boating Party.

This other French painter was a longtime owner of Renoir’s Moulin de la Galette, and that painting can be seen in this man’s self-portrait. He is perhaps more famous for The Floor Scrapers and Paris Street: Rainy Day.
Renoir also painted this Impressionist painter of The Boat During the Flood, Port-Marly. This English painter also created a series of paintings of the Thames.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This man created a Portrait of Painter Bazille and in his old age, he hired Richard Guino to follow his sculpting instructions. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this French painter of Luncheon of the Boating Party.

This other French painter was a longtime owner of Renoir's Moulin de la Galette, and that painting can be seen in this man's self-portrait. He is perhaps more famous for The Floor Scrapers and Paris Street: Rainy Day.
Renoir also painted this Impressionist painter of The Boat During the Flood, Port-Marly. This English painter also created a series of paintings of the Thames.

bonuses.leadin: This artist’s later pointillist works include Picking Peas and Potato Harvest, and he gained experience while studying under Anton Melbye. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist who painted day and night versions of Boulevard Montmartre. His Apple Picking at Eragny and Bather in the Woods both show women in a rural setting.
Pissarro exhibited as part of this movement in the middle of his career. It includes Manet and Monet, whose depiction of a sunrise scene shares its name.
This artist’s earliest surviving work is his Lane Near a Small Town. A British citizen, he is best known for works such as a View of the Thames and his Bridge at Villeneuve-la-Garenne.
